scope.purpose: Generic Bridge plumbing dependency shortlist only

method.version_source: Exact-version PyPI project JSON and release pages
method.source_provenance: Pinned upstream tag/commit; PyPI attestation where published; source-distribution SHA256 verified locally
method.license_verification: SHA256 of the license file in the official PyPI source distribution, byte-compared with the license at the pinned upstream commit
advisory_sources: GitHub Advisory Database REST query for ecosystem=pip and package@version
method.known_limitation: A zero-result advisory query is a time-bounded observation, not proof that a package is vulnerability-free. A fresh scan and exact transitive lock remain mandatory at adoption and release time.

stdlib_first.reason: The MVP needs a bounded administrative CLI, not a command framework.
Receipt and event schemas define structure; a logging dependency adds no required authority property.
SQLite is part of the pinned Python/runtime image and fits the single-host MVP.
These cover content addressing, constant-time comparison and random identifiers without custom cryptography.

stdlib_first.capability: Unix IPC and bounded process control
stdlib_first.selection: socket plus subprocess plus resource plus signal
stdlib_first.reason: The public Bridge has no HTTP-server requirement and the runner remains an offline child process/container.

stdlib_first.selection: os plus pathlib plus tempfile plus fcntl
stdlib_first.reason: The target MVP host is Linux; no cross-platform locking package is justified.

candidates.recommendation_boundary: Recommendation only. Agent 0 must issue a canonical ReuseDecisionReceipt and pin/audit every transitive dependency before installation.
candidates.role: Runtime validation of canonical JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 envelopes
candidates.optional_extras_policy: Do not install format or format-nongpl extras in the initial lock. Contract-specific date/time and identifier checks remain explicit until separately reviewed.
candidates.transitive_dependency_posture: MEDIUM: four direct runtime dependencies; rpds-py contains native Rust. Exact wheels, hashes, SPDX expressions and advisories are not frozen by this draft and are an adoption blocker.
candidates.fit: HIGH: canonical contracts already use JSON Schema and the package supports Draft 2020-12 without inventing a validator.
candidates.tcb: MEDIUM: approximately 11,163 Python/Rust/C/H lines across the unpacked sdist source footprint, plus four direct runtime dependencies.
candidates.maintenance: Production/stable; Python >=3.10 and published Python 3.14 support; release used PyPI trusted publishing with an attested source commit.
candidates.rollback: Remove the pinned package set and return to the existing contract-generation checks; retain the same canonical schemas and serialized envelopes.
adoption_conditions: exact transitive versions and hashes are locked
all transitive licenses and advisories are audited
negative tests prove unknown keywords, remote references and format assumptions fail closed
THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, LICENSES, provenance and SPDX SBOM are generated

candidates.recommendation_boundary: Reassess only when the separate typed connected model broker is implemented; never place it in the offline runner.
candidates.role: Bounded outbound HTTPS client for a future connected model broker
candidates.optional_extras_policy: No cli, http2, socks, brotli or zstd extras unless a later capability-specific receipt justifies them.
candidates.transitive_dependency_posture: MEDIUM: four unpinned direct dependencies and further transport dependencies. The candidate sdist has no PyPI provenance attestation, so commit-to-artifact provenance requires an independent release audit.
candidates.fit: MEDIUM later, NONE now: strict timeouts and pooling fit a connected broker, but Stage 0B has no connected executor implementation.
candidates.tcb: MEDIUM: approximately 17,751 Python source lines across the unpacked sdist source footprint, plus transport dependencies.
candidates.maintenance: The latest stable release is 0.28.1 from 2024 while 1.0 development prereleases exist; PyPI still classifies the project as beta.
candidates.rollback: Keep a narrow provider transport interface; replace HTTPX with another implementation or a test double without changing ModelCallEnvelope semantics.
adoption_conditions: exact transport dependency lock is audited
timeouts, proxy policy, redirect policy, TLS, response-size caps and retry ownership are tested
offline runner import graph proves HTTPX is absent

candidates.recommendation_boundary: Reject for the MVP Bridge contract layer; reconsider only with measured schema-binding value that cannot be met by dataclasses plus the canonical JSON Schemas.
candidates.role: Typed Python object validation and serialization
candidates.transitive_dependency_posture: HIGH: a compiled Rust core and three additional direct runtime packages create a second schema/serialization authority beside canonical JSON Schema.
candidates.fit: LOW: it is capable, but duplicates validation and serialization semantics that must remain schema-first and language-neutral.
candidates.tcb: HIGH: approximately 114,752 Python/Rust/C/H lines across the unpacked sdist source footprint, including the merged native core.
candidates.maintenance: Active, production/stable and attested, but high release velocity increases migration and semantic-drift work for no demonstrated MVP gain.
candidates.rollback: No adoption means no rollback is required. If reconsidered, generated bindings must remain disposable and never become the canonical schema source.

candidates.recommendation_boundary: Reassess only if an approved design requires asymmetric signatures or certificate handling not supplied by the deployment platform.
candidates.optional_extras_policy: Do not install the ssh extra.
candidates.transitive_dependency_posture: HIGH: native Rust/OpenSSL wheels plus CFFI on CPython; release and platform wheel selection are security-significant.
candidates.fit: LOW now: SHA-256, HMAC, random tokens, TLS and encrypted off-host backup are already covered by stdlib/runtime/deployment tools.
candidates.tcb: HIGH: approximately 110,732 Python/Rust/C/H lines across the unpacked sdist source footprint, native wheels and OpenSSL coupling.
candidates.maintenance: Mature and actively patched; its advisory history demonstrates why wheel/OpenSSL provenance and fast patching would become operational obligations.
candidates.rollback: Keep receipt authenticity abstracted from serialization. Remove the signing adapter and continue hash/ref integrity if policy permits.

external_components_rejected_for_mvp.reason: logging plus schema-defined JSON is sufficient.
Retries are durable state transitions with budgets and idempotency, not a function decorator.
The MVP uses a local Unix socket and exposes no HTTP server.
The frozen host is Linux and fcntl is sufficient.
